title: Dysplastic Nevus vs Low-CSD Melanoma
description: Diagnostic criteria atlas for evidence matching. category_id aligns with melanoma.json categories.

criteria.name: Symmetry
criteria.dysplastic_nevus: Roughly symmetric — bilateral silhouette, nest distribution, and pigmentation are approximately mirror images
criteria.melanoma_ssm: Asymmetric — architecture, cell density, and pigmentation differ between the two halves
criteria.explanation: First step at low power. Asymmetry is the most classic low-power clue for melanoma. Some melanomas can be symmetric (nevoid melanoma), so symmetry ≠ safe, but asymmetry always warrants concern.
criteria.computational_quantification: ①Extract patch features from each half.  ②Compute mean similarity of corresponding patches across midline.

criteria.name: Overall Growth Orientation
criteria.dysplastic_nevus: "East-west" orientation — thin and flat, extending horizontally along surface, shallow dermal component
criteria.melanoma_ssm: Horizontal + vertical — RGP (Radial Growth Phase) expands horizontally + VGP (Vertical Growth Phase) grows vertically into deep dermis
criteria.explanation: Dysplastic nevi go "east-west" (horizontal), congenital nevi go "north-south" (vertical). Both horizontal features AND deep atypical cell clusters → unlikely simple dysplastic nevus.
criteria.computational_quantification: ①Tissue segmentation → lesion extent   ②Cell segmentation → all melanocytes  ③Compute bounding box: width (W) and depth (D)  ④Aspect ratio = D / W. Dysplastic nevus: low ratio (wide, shallow). SSM with VGP: increased ratio. ⑤Compute melanocyte density distribution across depth layers: dysplastic nevus density drops rapidly with depth; SSM maintains high density at depth.

criteria.name: Pagetoid Spread
criteria.dysplastic_nevus: [+] Minimal or absent — may have "limited suprabasal scatter," only occasional cells centrally
criteria.melanoma_ssm: [-] Extensive, prominent — "buckshot" pattern: numerous single atypical melanocytes scattered throughout all epidermal layers, including the periphery
criteria.explanation: THE SINGLE MOST IMPORTANT FEATURE. Carefully search entire lesion for pagetoid spread. Pay special attention to the periphery — peripheral pagetoid spread is more alarming than central. Exception: Spitz/Reed nevi may show central pagetoid (stops abruptly at edges).
criteria.computational_quantification: ①Tissue segmentation → epidermal layers (basal, spinous, granular)  ②Cell segmentation + classification → all intraepidermal melanocytes  ③Divide into basal and suprabasal ④Pagetoid Index = suprabasal melanocyte count / total intraepidermal melanocyte count. ⑤Spatial analysis: Divide lesion into central 1/3 and peripheral 1/3 → calculate pagetoid index per zone → high peripheral index → strongly suggests malignancy. ⑥Depth distribution: melanocytes reaching granular layer → extremely alarming.

criteria.name: Confluent Growth
criteria.dysplastic_nevus: [-] Nests separated by normal keratinocytes; bridging nests are not solid continuous lines
criteria.melanoma_ssm: [+] Atypical melanocytes form continuous solid line replacing basal layer. "Like a child drawing a solid line with a crayon"
criteria.explanation: Continuous replacement of basal layer. Ben Wood technique: draw line connecting dermal papillae tops; melanocytes filling inter-rete space above = confluence. "Unzipped zipper" sign: severe confluence → epidermal-dermal clefting.
criteria.computational_quantification: ①Tissue segmentation → basal layer / DEJ (Dermo-Epidermal Junction)  ②Cell segmentation → melanocytes along DEJ  ③Linearize DEJ into 1D sequence ④DEJ melanocyte coverage = melanocyte-occupied DEJ length / total DEJ length. ⑤Longest continuous melanocyte segment: dysplastic nevi have gaps; SSM has long continuous stretches. ⑥Melanocyte density above vs. below papillae tip line → high ratio → favors malignancy.

criteria.name: Nest Location and Distribution
criteria.dysplastic_nevus: Nests at rete ridge tips or sides, bridging tip to tip. Nests relatively uniform in size.
criteria.melanoma_ssm: Nests of varying sizes, not confined to rete tips, any location, may coalesce
criteria.explanation: Normal nevi nest at rete tips. Dysplastic nevi have bridging but follow rules. Melanoma nests are everywhere, variable size, coalescing.
criteria.computational_quantification: ①Tissue segmentation → rete ridges and dermal papillae ②Cell segmentation → melanocyte nests  ③Label each nest: rete tip / rete side / inter-rete space / suprabasal  ④Rete tip nest proportion = rete tip nests / total nests. High → favors benign. ⑤Nest area Coefficient of Variation: low → uniform (dysplastic nevus); high → variable (melanoma). ⑥Nest coalescence detection: no keratinocyte gap between adjacent nests → coalesced. Calculate coalescence proportion.

criteria.name: Bridging & Lamellar Fibroplasia
criteria.dysplastic_nevus: [+] HALLMARK FEATURES — Nests bridge rete tip to rete tip forming "bridges," surrounded by dense lamellar/concentric fibroplasia ("onion skin" wrapping)
criteria.melanoma_ssm: [-] Not characteristic of melanoma — bridging + fibroplasia without pagetoid/confluence favors dysplastic nevus
criteria.explanation: [!] BEGINNER'S TRAP: Bridging and fibroplasia distinguish dysplastic nevus from ORDINARY NEVUS, NOT from melanoma!
criteria.computational_quantification: ①Tissue segmentation → rete ridges → ②Cell segmentation → nests spanning two rete tips → label as bridging nest → count/proportion. ③Fibroplasia detection: Extract patches around bridging nests → patch similarity retrieval vs. known fibroplasia templates. Or train a classifier. ④Bridging + fibroplasia co-occurring → supports dysplastic nevus.

criteria.name: Shouldering
criteria.dysplastic_nevus: [+] Junctional component extends beyond dermal component by ≥ 3 rete ridges
criteria.melanoma_ssm: [+] Also seen — in situ component extends beyond invasive component, not a diagnostic focus
criteria.explanation: CANNOT distinguish between the two — both can show it. Also common in congenital nevi. Quantitative threshold: ≥ 3 rete ridges.
criteria.computational_quantification: ①Cell segmentation → distinguish junctional vs. dermal melanocytes ②Calculate horizontal span of each ③Shoulder distance = junctional span − dermal span (each side)  ④Tissue segmentation → count rete ridges within this distance → ≥ 3 = positive. Note: cannot independently differentiate.

criteria.name: Dermal Maturation
criteria.dysplastic_nevus: [+] Maturation present — Type A (large epithelioid) → Type B (small round) → Type C (spindle). Nests shrink large → small → single cells.
criteria.melanoma_ssm: [-] Lack of maturation — Deep cells remain large with prominent nucleoli; large nests persist or enlarge (VGP (Vertical Growth Phase) expansile nests larger than intraepidermal)
criteria.explanation: MOST IMPORTANT criterion for dermal component malignancy. Look directly at deepest cells — large nuclei with prominent nucleoli at depth → no maturation → suspect melanoma.
criteria.computational_quantification: ①Tissue segmentation → dermis into superficial/mid/deep layers according to distance ②Cell segmentation per layer → extract morphological features (nuclear area, cytoplasmic area, N:C ratio, roundness, aspect ratio) ③Mean nuclear area and mean nest area per layer ④Nuclear area vs. depth curve → Maturation gradient = (superficial mean − deep mean) / superficial mean. Positive & large → maturation (benign); near 0 or negative → no maturation (malignant). ⑤Nest area vs. depth + cell density vs. depth → multi-metric. ⑥Morphological type transitions: large/round → small/spindle with depth → supports maturation.

criteria.name: Degree & Uniformity of Cytologic Atypia
criteria.dysplastic_nevus: Atypia present but limited and heterogeneous — nuclear enlargement, chromatin condensation, small-medium nucleoli. Uneven: some cells atypical, some normal.
criteria.melanoma_ssm: Severe and more uniform atypia — large nuclei, prominent nucleoli, coarse chromatin, irregular nuclear membranes, marked pleomorphism. More uniform.
criteria.explanation: "Atypia is subjective" — architectural features more reliable than cytology. Do not diagnose malignancy from a few large cells. Key: diffuse vs. focal, uniform vs. heterogeneous.
criteria.computational_quantification: ①Cell segmentation → nuclear morphological features: area, perimeter, roundness, eccentricity, chromatin texture, nucleolar area ②Compare with basal keratinocyte nuclear area → Nuclear size ratio = melanocyte mean / keratinocyte mean. ③Uniformity: CV of all melanocyte nuclear areas → low CV = uniform (melanoma); high CV = heterogeneous (dysplastic nevus). ④Patch-level retrieval vs. severe/mild atypia templates.

criteria.name: Dermal Mitoses
criteria.dysplastic_nevus: Rare — occasional single mitosis. WHO: when present, melanoma should enter the differential.
criteria.melanoma_ssm: Common — especially in VGP (Vertical Growth Phase). Atypical mitoses even more concerning.
criteria.explanation: BEST SINGLE DIFFERENTIATING FEATURE (PathologyOutlines exam answer). Focus on dermis. Gardner: mitoses are often "the last straw."
criteria.computational_quantification: ①Tissue segmentation → dermal region (exclude epidermis)   ②Run mitosis detection ③Dermal mitotic density = count / dermal area (per mm²). Density > 0 → concern; high → strongly suggests melanoma. ④Depth-stratified: deep mitoses more alarming → annotate depth of each. ⑤Atypical mitoses (tripolar, asymmetric) → additional weighting.

criteria.name: Epidermal Changes
criteria.dysplastic_nevus: Acanthosis — epidermal thickening, elongated rete ridges, basic architecture preserved
criteria.melanoma_ssm: Rete ridge effacement / epidermal attenuation — normal undulations disappear, epidermis flattens. WHO: NOT features of dysplastic nevi.
criteria.explanation: Easily overlooked but useful. Dysplastic nevi have normal rete ridge-papilla undulations; melanoma destroys this. SSM may show regression.
criteria.computational_quantification: ①Tissue segmentation → epidermis ②Sample epidermal thickness (stratum corneum to basal layer) ③Mean thickness + thickness CV → thin + low CV = effacement → melanoma. ④Rete ridge depth + spacing → Undulation index = rete ridge depth SD / mean epidermal thickness → low = effacement → melanoma. ⑤Regression detection: focal "no melanocytes + dermal fibrosis + melanophages" → patch retrieval with regression templates.

criteria.name: Extension Along Adnexal Structures
criteria.dysplastic_nevus: [-] Dysplastic nevi do not track down sweat ducts or follicular epithelium
criteria.melanoma_ssm: [+] In situ melanoma can extend down sweat duct and follicular epithelium
criteria.explanation: Very practical but easily overlooked. Melanocytes tracking down sweat duct epithelium in a "dysplastic nevus-like" lesion → strongly suspect melanoma.
criteria.computational_quantification: ①Tissue segmentation → adnexal structures (follicles, sweat ducts)  ②Cell segmentation → melanocytes within adnexal epithelium  ③Adnexal melanocyte density → markedly > 0 → adnexal extension → supports melanoma. ④Distinguish from congenital nevi: congenital = melanocytes around (outside) adnexa; melanoma = melanocytes infiltrate (inside) adnexal epithelium.

criteria.name: Anatomic Site & Clinical Context
criteria.dysplastic_nevus: Back, trunk, extremities. Young patients. Rarely face/scalp. Low-CSD skin.
criteria.melanoma_ssm: Male: back/shoulders. Female: legs. Low-CSD skin.
criteria.explanation: Gardner's RULE: Sun-damaged face/scalp in elderly → NEVER casually diagnose dysplastic nevus. Those lesions are almost always lentigo maligna.
criteria.computational_quantification: ①Clinical metadata: biopsy site, age, sex → direct features into classification model.

criteria.name: Pattern of Invasion
criteria.dysplastic_nevus: Gentle small-cell infiltration into collagen — deep type B/C cells, no inflammation/desmoplasia. Dermis "doesn't care."
criteria.melanoma_ssm: Expansile pushing nests — VGP (Vertical Growth Phase) nests larger than intraepidermal nests, with mitoses. Deep cells remain large and atypical. May have inflammation/fibrosis.
criteria.explanation: Benign = "quiet infiltration" (small cells slip between collagen). Malignant = "aggressive pushing" (large atypical clusters displace normal tissue). Deep solid nodules > epidermal nests = VGP = melanoma.
criteria.computational_quantification: ①Tissue segmentation → dermal layers  ②Cell segmentation → deepest melanocyte nests  ③Deep largest nest area vs. superficial/intraepidermal largest nest area ratio → ratio > 1 → VGP → melanoma. ④Stromal reaction: patches around deep nests → detect lymphocytic infiltration (cell classifier) → reaction = dermis "cares" → malignant invasion. ⑤Deep vs. superficial morphology → refer to #8 maturation.

usage_notes: Symmetry, pagetoid, confluent growth, maturation carry most weight. Prioritize architectural features over cytologic. Dermal mitoses (#10) is the most important in difficult cases.
